skill.name: markdown-url
skill.description: Routes web page URLs through markdown.new to create cleaner Markdown-friendly reading views for documentation, articles, and other read-only pages.
skill.summary: Convert public web pages into markdown.new reading URLs for cleaner extraction and summaries.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, hidden file, and weak-crypto alerts are false positives caused by Markdown formatting, URL examples, and ordinary text. The confirmed risk is intentional routing of requested web URLs through markdown.new, a third-party network service, so users should avoid private, authenticated, or sensitive URLs.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party URL Routing Privacy Risk
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally rewrites requested web URLs to use https://markdown.new/ before browsing. This is useful for read-only extraction, but it discloses target URLs to a third-party service and could expose sensitive page addresses if users ignore the documented exclusions for login, payment, authenticated, or cookie-dependent workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and instructions clearly route URLs through a third-party domain. The skill also documents exclusions for sensitive workflows, which lowers maliciousness but not privacy risk.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md alerts point to Markdown inline code, numbered instructions, and a fenced CLI example, not Ruby backtick execution. The only executable example is an explicit optional Node helper command shown to the user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown syntax and usage instructions rather than shell interpolation or automatic command execution. No user-controlled command is executed by these lines.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access Alert Is A URL Scheme Regex
low_findings.description: The detected filesystem pattern is a regular expression that rejects non-HTTP URL schemes. It does not read files, enumerate paths, or access hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is only a string validation regex inside normalizeToAbsoluteUrl. There is no filesystem API or path access near the finding.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto alerts are not cryptographic operations. The flagged lines contain frontmatter text, URL scheme wording, and ordinary prose about articles or fallback behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found no hashing, encryption, signing, or digest API usage. The static matches appear to come from short text substrings rather than crypto code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Third-Party Markdown Proxy
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper constructs markdown.new URLs by concatenating a fixed external service domain with the normalized destination URL. This is an intentional feature, but it is the primary security-relevant behavior of the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded external URL is directly visible in both the script and instructions. Its behavior is clear and does not depend on hidden code paths.

content.user_title: Convert Web Links to Markdown Views
content.value_statement: Web pages can be hard to read, summarize, or copy into notes. This skill rewrites suitable public URLs through markdown.new for cleaner Markdown-oriented extraction.
actual_capabilities: Rewrites HTTP and HTTPS URLs into markdown.new-prefixed URLs.
Normalizes bare domains and paths by adding an HTTPS scheme.
Guides agents to use markdown.new for read-only documentation, articles, changelogs, and GitHub discussions.
Defines fallback behavior when markdown.new is blocked, incomplete, or rate limited.
Includes a small Node.js helper that prints the converted URL.
limitations: It does not fetch or parse page content by itself.
It depends on the availability and behavior of markdown.new.
It should not be used for authenticated, payment, upload, or cookie-dependent workflows.
It rejects non-HTTP schemes such as mailto, file, and local relative paths.

use_cases.title: Read Documentation Cleanly
use_cases.description: Route public documentation pages through markdown.new before summarizing setup steps, API notes, or release changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Research Notes
use_cases.description: Turn public articles and guides into cleaner Markdown views for note-taking and citation review.

use_cases.title: Summarize Project Discussions
use_cases.description: Use Markdown-friendly views of public GitHub issues or pull requests when extracting decisions and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this public page to a markdown.new URL and show me the rewritten link: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this documentation through markdown.new when appropriate, then summarize the installation steps and important warnings: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Extract Release Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use a markdown.new view of this changelog if it works, then list the user-facing changes and migration risks: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Compare Public Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: For these public read-only URLs, try markdown.new once per source, fall back if blocked, and compare the main claims, dates, and unresolved questions: [URLS]

output_examples.input: Summarize a public changelog through markdown.new.
output_examples.output: The response provides a concise changelog summary, notes whether markdown.new worked, and calls out migration risks.

output_examples.input: Review a page that requires login.
output_examples.output: The response skips markdown.new, uses the original URL if browsing is needed, and explains that authenticated pages are excluded.

best_practices: Use the skill only for public, read-only pages where text extraction is the goal.
Try markdown.new once, then fall back quickly when content is blocked or incomplete.
Review extracted text against the original page when accuracy is important.
anti_patterns: Do not route login, payment, upload, dashboard, or authenticated URLs through markdown.new.
Do not use markdown.new for local files, private network addresses, or non-HTTP schemes.
Do not assume the Markdown view preserves every interactive element or hidden page section.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It rewrites suitable web URLs so an agent can open them through markdown.new for cleaner Markdown-style reading.

faq.question: Does it download page content?
faq.answer: No. The helper only prints a rewritten URL. The agent or browser performs any page visit.

faq.question: Is it safe for private links?
faq.answer: No. Private, authenticated, payment, and cookie-dependent URLs should use the original site instead.

faq.question: Which URLs are supported?
faq.answer: It supports HTTP and HTTPS URLs. Bare domains are normalized to HTTPS before adding the markdown.new prefix.

faq.question: What happens if markdown.new is blocked?
faq.answer: The instructions say to stop retrying and fall back to the original URL for browsing or text extraction.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
